purpose: Track deferred/incomplete acceptance criteria and known-by-inspection gaps from shipped phases. Reference this file when a converter / agent / pipeline regression surfaces — the answer may already be cataloged here. Add an entry whenever a phase ships with a soft acceptance criterion or a speculative gap that real-fixture data would close.

items.title: LEGO real-fixture validation of is_physical_card_surface
items.summary: 50.7 plan acceptance criterion #4 ('LEGO membership card section #16 detects 3+ signals -> is_physical=True') was not mechanically verified against real LEGO data. Detector is validated end-to-end only against synthetic DesignNode fixtures (test_lego_membership_card_pattern uses a hand-shaped tree).
code_refs: app/design_sync/figma/layout_analyzer.py:397-414 (wiring; only fires when inner_bg is not None)
items.symptom_if_broken: Phase 52.7's Rule 9 dark-mode flip will incorrectly invert the LEGO Insiders membership card (and other physical-card surfaces) in dark mode, because is_physical_card_surface returns False on real Figma extractions even though the card visually qualifies.
items.closes_when: Either (a) Phase 53.6 promotes LEGO into data/debug/lego/ as a regression case with manifest expectation 'sections[16].is_physical_card_surface: true', OR (b) Phase 51A pulls a slim 51.0 LEGO-promotion task forward as a prereq for 51.2/51.3/51.6.
items.fast_closure_path: Parse the cached raw_figma JSON via app.design_sync.figma.adapter.figma_to_design_node(), pass through analyze_layout(), assert sections[16].is_physical_card_surface is True. ~30-line one-off script — no live Figma PAT required.
items.closure_note: Phase 50.8 promoted LEGO into data/debug/7/ (real Figma extract, node 2833-1869). New test app/design_sync/tests/test_physical_card_detector.py::TestLEGORealFixture::test_lego_membership_card_detected_on_real_fixture loads structure.json, finds the membership card mj-section (corner_radius=18), and asserts detect_physical_card_surface() returns is_physical=True with signals=[barcode_child, distinct_corner_radius]. Test passes locally with the fixture present; matches the CI-skip convention for cases 5/6/10 (data/debug/*/structure.json is gitignored — pytest.skip when absent). Mechanical CI enforcement would require a fixture-hydration step (out of scope for 50.8). Closure path (a) taken.

items.title: _has_logo_on_white_field walks at most 2 nesting levels
items.summary: Detector finds a logo image only when nested as 'card -> white-frame -> image' or 'card -> white-frame' (image is the white frame itself). Real Figma exports often add a padding wrapper between the card and the white field, producing 'card -> padding-frame -> white-frame -> image'. Detector misses the logo signal in that shape.
items.symptom_if_broken: Physical cards with a wrapped-white logo field score one fewer signal than expected. With only 1 of 4 signals firing, is_physical=False, and Rule 9 incorrectly dark-flips them. Particularly likely on cards with explicit padding tokens applied at multiple nesting depths.
items.closes_when: Either (a) AC #4 closure surfaces this empirically and the fix lands as a follow-up, OR (b) someone writes a recursive walker mirroring _walk_image_descendants but bounded by depth (e.g. 4 levels) and adds a regression test with a real fixture.
items.fix_sketch: Replace the `for child in card.children` loop with a depth-bounded BFS that examines every FRAME with a white fill at any depth <=4, then checks its descendants for image children with area <= 60% of the white field's area.
items.closure_note: Speculation refuted by AC #4 closure. On the real LEGO fixture, detector returns is_physical=True via signals=[barcode_child, distinct_corner_radius] — without needing the logo_on_white_field signal. The single-level walk in _has_logo_on_white_field was not the limiting factor for this case. Recursive walker not required for LEGO; entry can be re-opened if a future fixture demonstrates a wrapped-white logo blocking detection.

items.title: Detection gated on inner_bg being set by 50.4
items.summary: Wiring at layout_analyzer.py:403 only runs detect_physical_card_surface when 50.4's _detect_inner_bg returns a non-None inner_bg. If the LEGO membership card is structured as a top-level section without an outer coloured wrapper (e.g. the card IS the entire footer area, not nested inside a coloured band), inner_bg=None and detection never fires — regardless of how many signals would have matched.
items.symptom_if_broken: Same as Gap 2: is_physical_card_surface=False on real cards even though the detector's signal logic would have classified them correctly if invoked.
items.closes_when: AC #4 closure determines whether LEGO's section #16 has inner_bg populated. If not, either (a) relax the gate to fire on any section with corner_radius >= 16 + barcode/QR descendant, OR (b) extend 50.4's _detect_inner_bg to populate inner_bg for top-level cards (section IS the card).
items.closure_note: Speculation partially confirmed but superseded. On LEGO, analyze_layout() does not pick up is_physical_card_surface=True on any of the 8 top-level sections — but the root cause is different from what gap-3 captured: the membership card is NESTED (depth=2) inside section[7]'s mj-wrapper, not a top-level card with inner_bg=None. Relaxing the inner_bg gate alone would not surface the LEGO case. The deeper structural issue (layout_analyzer needs subtree-walking for physical-card surfaces) is tracked as phase-50.8-nested-physical-cards.

items.title: pyright reportDeprecated demoted to warning to absorb @asynccontextmanager false-positive in 1.1.409
items.summary: pyright 1.1.409's bundled typeshed marks contextlib.asynccontextmanager / contextmanager as deprecated. Under strict mode (typeCheckingMode='strict') this fires reportDeprecated=error on every decorated generator and breaks `make types`. Workaround: pyproject.toml sets `reportDeprecated = 'warning'` instead of patching every call site or pinning pyright back. The narrower fix is to annotate each decorated function as `-> AsyncGenerator[T]` / `-> Generator[T]` (the typeshed deprecation only fires when the return type is a bare generator), which sidesteps the deprecation entirely without weakening the project-wide rule.
items.symptom_if_broken: A genuinely deprecated API (e.g. a Pydantic v3 / SQLAlchemy 2.x removal) lands in a future bump and pyright now logs it as a warning instead of failing `make types` — the regression slips through CI. Less severe but possible: another false-positive deprecation upstream goes unnoticed because the project-wide demote masks it.
items.closes_when: The 5 cited @asynccontextmanager / @contextmanager call sites annotate their return types as `AsyncGenerator[Foo]` / `Generator[Foo]` (or upstream typeshed reverts the deprecation marker), AND `reportDeprecated` returns to its strict-mode default (`error`).
items.fix_sketch: For each cited site, change `async def lifespan(app):` -> `async def lifespan(app) -> AsyncGenerator[None]:` and `def fixture():` -> `def fixture() -> Generator[Foo]:`. Then drop `reportDeprecated = 'warning'` from pyproject.toml:324 and re-run `make types` to confirm green under the strict default.
items.closure_note: Migrated 5 @(async)contextmanager call sites to (Async)Generator[..., None]; reportDeprecated restored to strict-mode error.

items.title: sfmc/sync_provider.py and adobe/sync_provider.py carry the F020/F024/F069 anti-patterns Plan 04 collapsed in service.py
items.summary: Plan 04 dedup'd app/connectors/{sfmc,adobe}/service.py into OAuthConnectorService (audit F020/F021/F024/F069 RESOLVED). The sister sync_provider.py files (195 LOC each, byte-equivalent except for service-name swaps) were not in scope and still carry: (1) `_token_cache: ClassVar[dict[str, tuple[str, float]]] = {}` — process-wide unbounded cache (F024 anti-pattern); (2) `hashlib.sha256(credentials['client_id'].encode()).hexdigest()[:16]` — truncated cache key with cross-vendor collision risk (F069 anti-pattern); (3) the same OAuth + token-refresh + 401-evict shape that lives in OAuthConnectorService. The audit's RESOLVED claims are accurate for the files cited (service.py:23-158, :30, :62) but the duplication just moved one file over.
code_refs: app/core/cache.py (LruWithTtl — the existing replacement for ClassVar caches)
items.symptom_if_broken: Two SFMC/Adobe customers with `client_id` values that share the same first 16 hex chars of SHA-256 will get each other's access tokens for the lifetime of the cache entry (cross-tenant token reuse). Process-wide cache also grows unbounded under high-tenant-count workloads — same memory-leak class as F024.
items.closes_when: Either (a) the two SyncProvider classes are migrated onto OAuthConnectorService (delete ~330 LOC, vendor sync providers shrink to ~30 LOC each like service.py did), OR (b) at minimum the local _token_cache is replaced with `LruWithTtl(maxsize=64)` and the cache key drops the [:16] truncation in favour of `f'{vendor}:{client_id}'`. Option (a) closes F020 properly across both code paths; option (b) is the surgical-fix bandaid.
items.fast_closure_path: Option (b) bandaid: ~10-line patch per file — replace `_token_cache: ClassVar[dict] = {}` with an instance `LruWithTtl(maxsize=64, default_ttl=3600)` initialized in `__init__`, drop the `hashlib.sha256(...).hexdigest()[:16]` and use the full client_id (prefixed by vendor name). Existing cache-hit/miss tests cover the call sites; only the storage shape changes.
items.notes: Discovered while verifying Plan 04 stayed shipped (2026-05-04 sweep). Plan 04's verification commands (`rg '_token_cache: ClassVar' app/connectors/`) returned 0 hits when Plan 04 shipped because sync_provider.py was either added later or the rg was scoped tighter — worth checking git blame before assuming it's a regression vs. a pre-existing miss.
items.closure_note: Closed via Option (b) — surgical bandaid in tech-debt/04-connector-oauth-cache. Replaced `_token_cache: ClassVar[dict]` + SHA-256[:16] cache key with per-instance `LruWithTtl(maxsize=64)` keyed by `f"{vendor}:{client_id}"` in app/connectors/{sfmc,adobe}/sync_provider.py. Dropped hashlib + time imports. Added cross-tenant + per-instance regression tests in app/connectors/{sfmc,adobe}/tests/test_sync_provider.py. Option (a) — full migration onto OAuthConnectorService — remains as future work; both files now carry a `# TODO(tech-debt):` marker pointing back to this entry so the next sweep knows the bandaid is intentional.

items.title: Pre-existing model<->DB schema drift surfaced by `alembic check` advisory step on every PR
items.summary: CI step `Migration safety (alembic upgrade + check)` is `continue-on-error: true`. It autogenerates a diff between the live DB (after `alembic upgrade head`) and SQLAlchemy models, and currently emits a 200+-line `New upgrade operations detected` block on every PR. The drift accumulated across multiple shipped phases — none of it caused by PR #98, all of it visible on main `a6f452bf` and earlier. Confirmed by checking gh run 25339533176 (main, pre-#98) which carries the identical annotation.
code_refs: .github/workflows/ci.yml (Migration safety job, alembic check step — advisory, continue-on-error: true)
app/example/ (deleted in eddcd1ac/F007) -- but no migration drops the `items` table

alembic/versions/ (~15 historical migrations declare TIMESTAMP() without tz on created_at/updated_at across blueprint_checkpoints, calibration_records, calibration_summaries, collaborative_documents, component_qa_results, design_connections, design_import_assets, design_imports, design_token_snapshots, esp_connections, rendering_screenshots, rendering_tests, etc.)
alembic/versions/ (PK index drift: ix_*_id missing on a dozen tables — most DBs auto-create, advisory still flags)
items.symptom_if_broken: Future `alembic revision --autogenerate` will mass-generate the accumulated drift as a single 200+-op migration if anyone runs it without first reading this entry — risk of accidentally landing a destructive migration that drops `items`, alters timestamp types in production, and adds NOT NULL constraints that break inserts where the column allows NULL today. Also: model contract (NOT NULL) doesn't match runtime contract (nullable in DB) on 5 tables, so an insert pattern that the model says is invalid may currently succeed in production, and tightening models to match the DB (or vice versa) will surface the inconsistency.
items.closes_when: Single migration rev that (a) drops `items` table + 3 indexes, (b) ALTER COLUMNs the ~30 timestamp columns to `TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE`, (c) adds NOT NULL constraints on the 10 affected created_at/updated_at columns (NULL backfill required first — verify count of NULL rows pre-migration), (d) reconciles the qa_overrides unique constraint, memory_entries HNSW index, and design_connections/design_token_snapshots column comments. After landing, `alembic check` should exit 0 and the CI annotation goes away. Belongs in Session 19 (backend sweep) alongside F057 migration squash.
items.fast_closure_path: Run `alembic revision --autogenerate -m 'reconcile model<->schema drift'` against a fresh DB at head, hand-review the generated ops (split into multiple revs if too large), null-backfill the columns that need NOT NULL before applying. Squawk gate (Migration safety squawk job) will catch unsafe ALTER COLUMNs — expect to add `op.execute('SET lock_timeout = ...')` guards.
items.notes: The advisory check has been failing-as-non-blocking on every PR for some time. Surfaced as a question on PR #98 (https://github.com/linardsb/merkle-email-hub/pull/98). NOT a regression introduced by recent work. Tracked here so a future PR doesn't autogenerate it as a destructive migration without realizing the scope.

items.title: Cross-entity tenant-isolation regression test self-skips — no integration DB harness
items.summary: Plan 03 §C calls for a load-bearing cross-org regression test (`app/tests/test_tenant_isolation.py`) that spins up two orgs/users via a real Postgres `db: AsyncSession` fixture and asserts no entity (projects, templates, memory, qa_results, approvals) leaks across orgs. The scoping logic itself (F001/F002) is enforced — every route is on `get_scoped_db`, every repo calls `scoped_access`, and a forgotten swap raises `RuntimeError` (caught by every existing route test). What's missing is the integration harness: there is no project-wide `db: AsyncSession` fixture with table truncation. The test self-skips when `TEST_DATABASE__URL` is unset; once the harness lands the test activates, with `xfail(strict=True)` on templates/memory/qa_results/approvals so they fail-loud and prompt promotion to plain `id=...`.
code_refs: app/tests/test_tenant_isolation.py:48 (db fixture self-skips on no TEST_DATABASE__URL)
conftest.py:88 (_override_scoped_db autouse opts out on tenant_isolation marker — harness will drop straight in)
items.symptom_if_broken: A future repo lands without `scoped_access` filtering and the regression net does not catch it because no integration test ever runs. The fail-loud `RuntimeError` on missing `tenant_access` covers the 'forgot to swap get_db' case but NOT the 'wrote a new repo with no scope filter' case — that's exactly what this regression test exists to catch.
items.closes_when: A `db: AsyncSession` fixture lands (likely under `app/tests/conftest.py` or root conftest) that creates an isolated Postgres schema per test (or truncates between tests), AND `app/tests/test_tenant_isolation.py` runs green in CI without `TEST_DATABASE__URL` gating. Promote the four `xfail(strict=True)` entries (templates/memory/qa_results/approvals) to plain `id=...` once each entity's payload schema is wired.
items.fast_closure_path: Smallest viable harness: a session-scoped engine pointed at a disposable test DB + a function-scoped `db` fixture that opens an `AsyncSession` and `TRUNCATE`s the relevant tables on teardown. Reuse `seed_org`/`seed_user` from `app/tests/factories.py`. Once the fixture exists, the test activates and the four xfail entities can be wired one at a time.
items.notes: F003 is independently closed by `db953681`'s shipped BOLA test (`app/projects/tests/test_bola.py`) and is unaffected by this gap. Briefs is intentionally `xfail(strict=False)` because it needs a user-isolation variant rather than the org-isolation pattern — track separately if it warrants its own entry. — Closed by tech-debt/03-tenant-iso-harness: app/tests/conftest.py provides session-scoped _integration_engine (alembic upgrade head) + per-test db fixture (TRUNCATE RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE); test_tenant_isolation.py wires templates/memory/qa_results/approvals via ENTITY_FIXTURES (EntitySpec dataclass with optional pre_seed for build/template_version dependencies); xfail(strict=True) dropped from those four; briefs stays xfail(strict=False) — user-isolation variant tracked under a new entry.

items.title: mypy 2.0 redundant-cast suppression on Figma style-run cast
items.closed_note: Closed by Option (a) — TypedDict boundary at app/design_sync/figma/raw_types.py types characterStyleOverrides as list[Any]; the redundant cast and # type: ignore[redundant-cast] in _parse_style_runs are gone. All 8 redundant-cast suppressions in service.py removed.
items.summary: mypy 2.0's improved isinstance narrowing flags `cast(list[Any], overrides)` in app/design_sync/figma/service.py:_parse_style_runs as `redundant-cast` after the `isinstance(overrides, list)` guard. Pyright still narrows the same value to `list[Unknown]` and would error without the cast. Workaround: keep the cast and tag the line with `# type: ignore[redundant-cast]` plus a 2-line comment. The narrower fix is to either (a) tighten the upstream `node_data` typing so `.get('characterStyleOverrides', [])` returns `list[Any]` directly (no cast needed by either checker), or (b) wait for pyright's isinstance narrowing to match mypy 2.0's, at which point the cast becomes truly redundant and the suppression can be deleted.
code_refs: app/design_sync/figma/service.py:152 (isinstance guard whose narrowing mypy now trusts but pyright doesn't)
pyproject.toml:88 (mypy>=2.0.0 — the bump that introduced the divergence)
items.symptom_if_broken: A future genuinely-redundant cast elsewhere in the codebase slips through because reviewers see the existing `# type: ignore[redundant-cast]` precedent and copy it. Less severe but possible: pyright catches up and the suppression silently does nothing — easy to miss without a tracker.
items.closes_when: Either (a) `_parse_style_runs` is refactored so `overrides` is typed as `list[Any]` at the call site (no cast needed by mypy or pyright), OR (b) pyright's isinstance narrowing matches mypy 2.0's behavior and removing both the cast and the ignore-comment leaves `make types` green.
items.fix_sketch: Option (a): change `node_data: dict[str, Any]` to a TypedDict with `characterStyleOverrides: NotRequired[list[Any]]`, then `overrides = node_data.get(...)` is already `list[Any]` and the isinstance guard suffices for both checkers. Option (b): drop the cast + `# type: ignore[redundant-cast]` line and run `uv run pyright app/design_sync/figma/service.py` to confirm pyright accepts the narrowing.

items.title: squawk migration-lint job is advisory because squawk 2.x does not parse Python alembic migrations
items.summary: The CI Migration safety (squawk) job invokes squawk directly on alembic .py files. squawk 2.51+ is a pure-SQL linter — every op.execute() string literal triggers a syntax error and the job exits 123 with 500+ findings. Demoted to continue-on-error in tech-debt-19. The squawk-ignore: column-type-change comments in normalize_schema_drift.py exist for the day this becomes useful again.
code_refs: Makefile (migration-lint target — same brokenness on local)
alembic/versions/normalize_schema_drift.py (squawk-ignore comments awaiting a linter that respects them)
items.symptom_if_broken: Future migrations with unsafe DDL (table-rewriting ALTER, missing IF EXISTS, naked DROP COLUMN on populated tables) ship without an automated check. The risky-pattern catch lives only in human review until a Python-aware migration linter (e.g. SQL-string AST extraction + feed to squawk, or a new tool) is wired in.
items.closes_when: A migration linter (Python-aware) reports zero findings on the current alembic/versions/ tree, AND the migration-lint job is promoted back to a hard gate (continue-on-error removed).
items.fast_closure_path: Write a tiny Python preprocessor that AST-walks alembic/versions/*.py, extracts every op.execute("...") + op.create_table/alter_column kwargs as raw SQL, writes to a tempfile, and feeds that to squawk. Replace the direct .py invocation with this preprocessor in CI + Makefile + pre-commit hook.
items.notes: Surfaced when normalize_schema_drift hit the squawk job in PR #133 — first PR with a non-trivial Python migration since the job was added in #101.

items.title: Briefs need a user-isolation (BOLA-by-creator) regression test, not org-isolation
items.summary: The cross-entity tenant-isolation harness (closed in tech-debt-03-tenant-isolation-regression-harness) parametrizes briefs as xfail(strict=False) because briefs are scoped by creator (BOLA-by-creator), not by org. The org-isolation pattern in test_no_cross_org_read does not apply: two users in the same org could still leak each other’s briefs. A user-isolation variant — same org, different creators, GET-by-id and list must filter by user — is needed for the same fail-loud net the other entities now have.
items.symptom_if_broken: A repo or route change drops creator-based filtering on briefs; one user in an org reads another user’s briefs and no regression test catches it (only the existing scoped_access fail-loud covers the missing-filter shape — not user-id-scoped leaks within an org).
items.closes_when: A test like test_no_cross_user_read_briefs lands under app/tests/, seeds two users in the SAME org, has user1 create a brief, asserts user2 cannot GET it by id and that user2’s list excludes it. Wire it into the integration harness alongside test_no_cross_org_read.
items.closed_note: Closed by app/tests/test_briefs_user_isolation.py — same-org two-user integration test asserting (1) repository get_connection returns None for a non-owner, (2) GET /connections list excludes another user's rows, (3) GET /connections/{id}/items, POST /connections/sync, and GET /items/{id} all 404 for a non-owner. Building the test surfaced a live fail-open BOLA leak: GET /api/v1/briefs/items/{id} returned another user's item because get_item_with_details is unscoped and get_item_detail's connection guard fell through when get_connection returned None for the non-owner. Fixed in app/briefs/service.py (raise BriefItemNotFoundError when conn is None, mirroring list_items_for_connection/sync_connection). Red→green proof runs in CI's integration job (make test-integration; docker-gated, unavailable at authoring time). closed_commit pending commit.
items.follow_up: Sibling unscoped read found while authoring: import_items → get_items_by_ids (app/briefs/repository.py:209) has no created_by_id filter; a user may import another user's brief items (only destination-project access is verified). Out of this entry's closes_when scope — filed as tech-debt-03-briefs-import-items-unscoped-read.
items.notes: Out of scope for tech-debt/03-tenant-iso-harness per that plan §“Out of scope”.

items.title: import_items reads brief items unscoped (get_items_by_ids has no owner filter) — cross-user BOLA
items.summary: BriefService.import_items fetches source items via BriefRepository.get_items_by_ids, which runs select(BriefItem).where(BriefItem.id.in_(ids)) with no ownership scoping. Brief ownership is item -> connection_id -> BriefConnection.created_by_id, and get_connection already applies scoped_access creator filtering — but get_items_by_ids bypasses it entirely. import_items only calls _verify_access on the DESTINATION project; it never verifies the caller owns the SOURCE items. Found while authoring app/tests/test_briefs_user_isolation.py, sibling to the get_item_detail fail-open fixed in the same branch. Current blast radius is limited: import_items today only resolves the project, logs, and returns ImportResponse(project_id) — it does not yet copy item content/resources/attachments. The missing gate becomes a real cross-user data leak the moment import is implemented to persist source content.
code_refs: app/briefs/repository.py:209 (get_items_by_ids — unscoped select, no connection-owner filter)
items.symptom_if_broken: A user supplies brief_item_ids owned by another creator (same or different org) to POST import; the items resolve with no ownership 404 and, once import persists content, another user's brief items/resources/attachments land in the caller's project. No regression test catches the cross-user read.
items.closes_when: get_items_by_ids (or import_items before consuming its result) scopes by the caller's ownership — join BriefItem.connection -> BriefConnection.created_by_id via scoped_access, mirroring get_connection — so non-owned IDs are dropped/404'd, AND a test in app/tests/test_briefs_user_isolation.py asserts user2 cannot import user1's brief items.
items.fix_sketch: Add an owner/scope param to get_items_by_ids and apply .join(BriefConnection).where(BriefConnection.created_by_id == access.user_id) when scoped_access is active; then in import_items raise BriefItemNotFoundError if the returned set doesn't cover every requested id (mirror the get_item_detail fix shape).
items.closed_note: Closed by scoping BriefRepository.get_items_by_ids to the caller's own connections (app/briefs/repository.py — join BriefConnection + created_by_id == access.user_id when scoped_access is active, mirroring list_items) plus a full-coverage guard in BriefService.import_items (app/briefs/service.py — raise BriefItemNotFoundError when the owner-scoped result doesn't cover every requested id via len(items) != len(set(brief_item_ids)), so non-owned/missing ids 404 instead of being silently dropped; mirrors the get_item_detail fix). import_items is the only caller of get_items_by_ids. The fix_sketch's owner/scope param was not used — every other BriefRepository method reads scoped_access(self.db) internally, so the param would have been inconsistent. Tests: app/tests/test_briefs_user_isolation.py adds test_repository_get_items_by_ids_filters_by_creator (non-owner scope returns []) and test_import_items_denies_other_user (POST /import — user2 404 at the item-ownership gate, user1 422 at the project lookup, same body); app/briefs/tests/test_service.py adds TestImportItems.test_missing_or_nonowned_ids_raise_not_found (service-guard unit, runs without a DB). Repo SQL scoping proof runs in CI's integration job (make test-integration; docker-gated, collect-skips locally at authoring time). closed_commit records the branch ref (the feature branch is squash-merged to main, so the eventual main SHA differs), matching the other closed entries in this ledger.

items.title: Memory row in tenant-isolation harness is xfail because POST /memory/ needs an embedding provider

items.title: analyze_layout doesn't run physical-card detector on nested subtrees
items.closure_note: layout_analyzer.py now falls back to find_physical_card_in_subtree() (physical_card_detector.py) when _detect_inner_bg returns inner_bg=None. The walker is bounded (max_depth=4), computes sibling radii per-candidate from the candidate's immediate parent, and requires the candidate to carry corner_radius >= 16 — without that precondition aspect_ratio_boarding_pass + barcode_child fire coincidentally on plain hero sections (a 600x67 banner strip image inside a 600x467 hero matches both). Asymmetry vs the top-level path is intentional: top-level detection runs only after _detect_inner_bg gates on a card-coloured surface, so the structural gate is doing the work; the subtree walker has no such gate and must use corner_radius as the visual hallmark. When a nested card is detected the parent section's physical_card_signals are prefixed with 'nested_card' for provenance. Trade-off: is_physical_card_surface remains a binary section-level flag, so any non-card siblings inside the same mixed wrapper inherit the flag; Phase 52.7 Rule 9 inherits this and will need to thread detection down to descendant-node granularity to correct dark-mode flips on non-card content. Validated by app/design_sync/tests/test_layout_analyzer.py::TestNestedPhysicalCardDetection on LEGO data/debug/7 (only sections[7] flagged; cases 5/6/8/9/10 unchanged at zero flagged) and app/design_sync/tests/test_physical_card_detector.py::TestSubtreeWalker (synthetic coverage for depth bound, corner-radius precondition, min_signals plumbing, per-candidate sibling radii — independent of the gitignored fixture).
items.summary: layout_analyzer.py:397-414 runs detect_physical_card_surface only on top-level section candidates. For real Figma campaigns where the physical-card surface is nested inside an outer mj-wrapper (e.g. LEGO data/debug/7: pages[0].children[0].children[7].children[2] is an mj-section with corner_radius=18 sitting inside a column-layout-4 footer wrapper), the detector is never invoked. detect_physical_card_surface() invoked directly on the card returns is_physical=True with 2 signals — the detection logic is correct, only the production wiring misses nested cards.
code_refs: app/design_sync/figma/layout_analyzer.py:397-414 (detector wiring — invokes only on top-level section)
app/design_sync/figma/layout_analyzer.py:502 (_get_section_candidates — defines what counts as a top-level section)
data/debug/7/structure.json (LEGO — real fixture demonstrating the nested case)
items.symptom_if_broken: Phase 52.7's Rule 9 dark-mode flip will incorrectly invert the LEGO Insiders membership card (and any other nested physical card) in dark mode, because is_physical_card_surface is False on the parent section even though a descendant of that section IS a physical card.
items.closes_when: layout_analyzer (or a new pre-pass) walks every top-level section's subtree (bounded depth e.g. 4 levels) and ORs the per-descendant detect_physical_card_surface result up to the section, OR the LEGO membership card surface gets reclassified as its own top-level section by a new section-discovery heuristic.

items.title: Squash scripts produce empty baseline migration (squash design flaw)
items.summary: scripts/squash-migrations.sh and scripts/squash-migrations-dryrun.sh both run alembic revision --autogenerate against the SAME DB that has just had all migrations applied. Autogenerate diffs target_metadata (Python models) against the live DB schema; when they match — which they always do at this point in the flow, by construction — the generated baseline migration is empty (def upgrade(): pass, zero op.create_table statements). Stamping head against this empty migration leaves the existing populated DB unchanged, but any future fresh-DB bootstrap will run an empty upgrade() and produce no schema. The runbooks (.agents/plans/tech-debt-19-runbook-db-squash.md procedure step 5) inherit the same flaw — alembic check after step 5 still passes deceptively (schema matches models regardless of whether the baseline contains anything).
code_refs: scripts/squash-migrations.sh:102-114 (mv versions/ then autogenerate then stamp — same-DB autogenerate)
Makefile:269-270 (db-squash target shells out to squash-migrations.sh)
TODO.md (§50.5 currently tagged [Plan Ready] — the plan is design-broken)
items.symptom_if_broken: After make db-squash cutover, alembic_version points at a baseline_squash_YYYYMMDD revision whose upgrade() body is `pass`. Production DB is unaffected (schema already exists). Symptom appears on the next fresh-DB deploy — CI staging spin-up, contributor onboarding, disaster-recovery restore — when `alembic upgrade head` against an empty DB runs the empty migration to completion with no tables created. First request that touches the DB raises psycopg/asyncpg UndefinedTable / relation does not exist.
items.closes_when: Both squash scripts (destructive + dry-run) generate the baseline via autogenerate against an EMPTY throwaway DB (not the populated one). Verified by inline assertion: grep -c "op.create_table" on the generated baseline file must equal the number of tables in target_metadata. The dry-run additionally applies that baseline to a third empty DB, captures pg_dump, and diffs it against the all-migrations-applied dump from the first DB — the diff must be empty.
items.fix_sketch: Three-DB flow. DB-A: docker run pgvector → alembic upgrade head → pg_dump schema_A. DB-B: docker run pgvector (empty) → alembic revision --autogenerate (compares models vs empty DB → baseline contains every CreateTable). DB-C: docker run pgvector (empty) → apply only the new baseline → pg_dump schema_C. Assert diff schema_A vs schema_C is empty (modulo alembic_version table). The current single-DB design conflates the source-of-schema and the autogenerate-target — they must be separated.
items.notes: Discovered 2026-05-25 by direct test: applied all 47 migrations to a throwaway pgvector container, truncated alembic_version, ran alembic revision --autogenerate against a temp tree with empty versions/. Generated file contained `def upgrade(): pass` with zero op.create_table statements. The destructive scripts/squash-migrations.sh has the same defect AND its operator-facing alembic check in step 6 of the runbook still passes (because schema matches models regardless of baseline content), so the runbook gives no warning. F057 (TODO.md §50.5) currently tagged [Plan Ready] — re-tag and do not execute make db-squash until this is closed. Two unrelated bugs in squash-migrations-dryrun.sh were fixed in the same investigation (hardcoded port 55555, host pg_dump major-version mismatch) — see follow-up commit.

items.title: Per-run typography (remove first-heading/body break) needs per-node <td> anchors — structural sub-project
items.summary:
Only the FIRST heading + FIRST body per non-column section get design typography; a break in component_matcher._build_token_overrides limits it. Removing the break in isolation is no-op-to-harmful: all overrides collide on the shared _heading/_body class (last-write-wins). The real fix is structural — stop the '

'.join in _fills_text_block, emit one data-node-id per text node, add a _text_ override target + renderer dispatch arm (mirror the existing _image_ pattern). style_runs is the WRONG lever (intra-node char spans, not per-node typography).

items.title: MemoryService.recall() is dead-on-read — every recall raises on an unstamped session and silently returns []
items.summary: The per-tenant DB-scoping fix (F001/F002/F003, db953681) made MemoryRepository.similarity_search call scoped_access(self.db) unconditionally (repository.py:56); scoped_access raises RuntimeError on any session not stamped with tenant_access (scoped_db.py:176-181, 'failing loud is the point'). But every MemoryService.recall() caller opens a plain get_db_context() session, which does NOT stamp tenant_access (database.py:74-87) — unlike get_system_db_context (scoped_db.py:161-164). So every recall raises, each caller's failure-safe try/except swallows it, and recall returns []. The entire app/memory recall-into-context layer (blueprint LAYER 10 memory block, scaffolder conversion-memory recall, proactive_qa, correction_examples, insight_bus, confidence_calibration) is silently inert at read time despite memory.enabled=True and despite writes succeeding (writers that use get_system_db_context, e.g. converter quality memory, persist fine). Because the exception is swallowed, existing tests stay green and no integration test exercises the real scoped_access path on recall. Net: cross-task episodic + procedural memory never reaches an agent prompt — 'persistent memory across tasks' silently no-ops.
code_refs: app/core/scoped_db.py:176-181 (scoped_access raises RuntimeError on unstamped session)
app/core/scoped_db.py:161-164 (get_system_db_context — DOES stamp; the working pattern used by converter quality-memory writes)
app/ai/blueprints/engine.py:1501 (recall caller opens get_db_context — blueprint LAYER 10)
items.symptom_if_broken: Agents never recall prior-run memories: blueprint LAYER 10 memory block always empty; the converter->scaffolder quality-memory loop (writes succeed via get_system_db_context, reads return []) never surfaces 'avoid template X for client Y' hints; metrics/logs show memory rows being WRITTEN while recall yields nothing, and no exception is visible (swallowed).
items.closes_when: Recall callers open a tenant-stamped session (get_scoped_db / get_system_db_context) OR similarity_search accepts an explicit access scope instead of reading session.info — AND a test exercises the real scoped_access path (stop swallowing/mocking) so a regression re-surfaces. Confirm a blueprint run actually injects a recalled memory into a node prompt.
items.available_data: Discovered 2026-06-05; full trace in docs/agentic_rl_memory_handoff_findings.md sec 3.2 / 7.2 (5-agent audit, mechanism + caller side hand-verified). Distinct from closed tech-debt-03-memory-isolation-embedding-stub (an embedding-provider xfail in the isolation harness).
items.fix_sketch: Smallest fix: swap the recall-site session openers from get_db_context() to get_system_db_context() (system sentinel — correct for background/agent context that legitimately spans projects), mirroring how the converter quality-memory WRITE already works. Alternative: thread the caller's TenantAccess explicitly into similarity_search(..., access=...) and drop the session.info read.
items.notes: Prerequisite (B1) for any agent self-improvement loop that depends on memory recall (handoff_memory, insight_bus) — see proposed Phase 54. Also verify a separately-flagged gap: MemoryCompactionPoller appears never registered in app/main.py (decay/compaction never runs) — relevant before Phase 51.2 hardens compaction and before turning memory writes up (unbounded growth = write-only-memory anti-pattern). CLOSED 2026-06-11 (commit 0b0313aa): swapped all 15 background/agent memory-op openers from get_db_context() to get_system_db_context() (system sentinel) — recall reads (engine LAYER 10, scaffolder pipeline, proactive_qa, correction_examples, judge_aggregator, insight_bus, failure_patterns, knowledge agent service) + stores (same four, plus THREE sites the original scoping omitted: handoff_memory [handoff->episodic bridge], outcome_logger [blueprint_outcome rows that feed confidence_calibration], design_sync service snapshot store). Two corrections to this entry: (1) writes are dead only for project_id!=None stores — store() gates on scoped_access only when project_id is not None (service.py:37-38); create() itself never calls scoped_access (the cited "create:30" is actually get_by_id). (2) confidence_calibration needed NO change: its only live caller is the blueprint route passing a stamped get_scoped_db session (recall already worked); the workflow caller passes db=None, which skips calibration. Scaffolder conversion recall kept project_id=None (cross-project procedural learning, user-confirmed). Guard: app/tests/test_memory_recall_integration.py — first @integration + @tenant_isolation memory test (opts out of the autouse scoped_access bypass to exercise the REAL guard); engine._recall_memories proven RED pre-fix (recalled==[]), GREEN post. Six unit-test opener patches repointed get_db_context->get_system_db_context. Still open (separate): MemoryCompactionPoller appears unregistered in app/main.py — relevant before turning memory writes up (unbounded growth).

items.title: B5 'alt="" for decorative images' conflicts with the G3-neg conformance gate, which forbids empty/generic alt
items.summary: B1 (seed-literal cleanup) attempted to blank the image-grid seed's placeholder alt (alt="Grid image 1/2") to alt="". The golden-conformance gate G3-neg (test_golden_conformance.py:72-76, should_match=False) rejects alt values that are empty or a single generic word (image/photo/picture/img/mj-image/mj-text/frame/banner) — and correctly so here: image-grid's images are wrapped in <a href>, so an empty alt strips the link's accessible name. B1 therefore reverted the image-grid edit (restored the descriptive multi-word placeholder, matching sibling seeds full-width-image/article-card/logo-header, all of which pass G3-neg) and shipped only its gate-clean parts. The real placeholder-alt fix is deferred to B5 (alt derivation: real alt when available, alt="" only for genuinely decorative). But B5's prescribed alt=""-for-decorative re-trips this same gate the moment a decorative image flows through the fragment/snapshot conformance conversion.

items.notes: Surfaced during B1 execution (2026-06-06). DECISION 2026-06-06 (.agents/plans/53-b5-alt-derivation-decision.md): empirical trace confirms B5's column-builder path (_build_column_fill_html) IS exercised by golden-conformance (6 seeds) — so alt="" there WOULD fail the gate; but the leak only occurs on the Figma-fixture provenance of node_name (= node.name), which the data/debug regression does NOT alt-check. items.title: B8 cta-pair buttons render in seed-default color — the primary button's Figma color regresses vs the single-CTA path
items.summary: B8 routes CTA-typed sections with >=2 buttons to the cta-pair seed and _fills_cta emits primary_/secondary_ text+url fills. But the renderer's _cta token-override helpers (_replace_cta_background_color/_fillcolor/_text_color/_css_prop, component_renderer.py:1071-1149) match ONLY class="cta-btn"/"cta-ghost" or <a data-slot="cta_url">. The cta-pair seed uses class="cta" and data-slot="primary_url"/"secondary_url", so the _cta overrides _build_token_overrides extracts from buttons[0] (~1709) are no-ops on cta-pair output. Both buttons therefore render with the seed's hardcoded #e84e0f orange. This is a REGRESSION for the primary button specifically: the single-button cta-button path DID apply its Figma color; the dual path does not. A single TokenOverride targeting one _cta class also cannot express the distinct primary(filled)/secondary(outlined) colors the seed is designed for.
code_refs: app/design_sync/component_matcher.py:_fills_cta (B8 pair fills) + match_section CTA branch (~239, len(buttons)>=2 -> cta-pair routing)
email-templates/components/cta-pair.html (primary filled bg #e84e0f, secondary outlined; slots primary_text/primary_url/secondary_text/secondary_url)
items.symptom_if_broken: A Figma CTA section with two buttons of distinct brand colors renders both as the seed-default orange in email output; the primary button in particular loses the color it would have had as a single CTA.
items.closes_when: _cta token-override extraction + apply support per-button color for cta-pair: either (a) the _CTA_*_RE templates also match primary_url/secondary_url + class="cta", with _build_token_overrides emitting per-button overrides (e.g. _cta_primary/_cta_secondary target classes), or (b) the cta-pair seed is restructured to carry the cta-btn/cta_url markers the existing helpers target. Verified by a dual-CTA section with two distinct fill_colors rendering each button in its own color.
items.notes: Discovered during B8 execution (2026-06-06). Knowingly-shipped regression: the user chose to ship cta-pair activation (zero current-fixture impact — no fixture has a CTA-typed section) for correct infra + the real-world dual-CTA case. Per-button color fidelity deferred as a larger change (new regexes + per-button override model). Related: phase-53-b8-non-cta-multibutton-drop. CLOSED 2026-06-10 (b50dcf13) via approach (a): added cta-primary/cta-secondary classes to the seed + a shared _cta_overrides() helper that, for >=2 buttons, emits per-button _cta_primary/_cta_secondary overrides applied by a block-scoped _apply_cta_pair_override() in component_renderer.py. Approach (b) alone was a dead end — the existing _cta helpers are global (re.sub, no count), so marking both buttons cta-btn would paint both the same color; distinct per-button color needs the scoped per-button model regardless. background-color paints the bgcolor attr + the border shorthand (the outlined secondary has no bgcolor, so its border is its only color surface; an explicit stroke override still wins via emission order). Guard: TestDualCTAColorFidelity (full match_section->render_section, per-block color isolation, proven RED pre-fix). Zero corpus impact (cases 5-10 byte-identical). Siblings phase-53-b8-non-cta-multibutton-drop / -fills-cta-slug-desync-vlm remain open.

items.title: Non-CTA-typed multi-button sections still drop button 2+ — B8's cta-pair routing only fires on EmailSectionType.CTA
items.summary: B8 routes only EmailSectionType.CTA sections with >=2 buttons to cta-pair. Empirically the 6 converter regression fixtures (data/debug cases 5-10) contain ZERO CTA-typed sections, so B8 fires on none of them. The real multi-button defects sit in other section types: case 10 sec#1 is a 'content'-typed section classified as text-block (2 texts + 2 buttons) whose second button ('DISCOVER EIGER EXTREME 6.0') is DROPPED — _fills_text_block (~1040) emits buttons[0] only and text-block has a single CTA slot. (Most other fixture multi-button sections route to column-layout-N, which already render every button as a column — confirmed: case 5 output contains Melbourne/Amsterdam/Seoul — and are NOT affected.) Fixing the text-block drop is a classification/segmentation question (re-split into 2 sections? add a 2nd CTA slot? route button-dominant content to cta-pair, losing the texts?), deferred to the segmentation track (C/D) per the phase thesis that segmentation is the dominant defect.
code_refs: app/design_sync/component_matcher.py:match_section CTA branch (~239 — routing gated on EmailSectionType.CTA only)
data/debug/10 (case 10 sec#1: content-typed text-block, 2 buttons; 2nd label 'DISCOVER EIGER EXTREME 6.0' verified absent from rendered HTML)
items.symptom_if_broken: A content/hero/text-block section carrying 2+ buttons renders only the first; case 10 output lacks 'DISCOVER EIGER'. Any non-CTA-typed multi-button design loses buttons silently.
items.closes_when: Either the boundary/layout classifier types button-dominant multi-button sections as CTA (so B8's routing catches them), or the segmentation track splits/re-types case 10 sec#1 so both buttons render. Verified by case 10 rendered output containing BOTH 'SHOP THE COLLECTION' and 'DISCOVER EIGER EXTREME 6.0'.
items.notes: Discovered during B8 execution (2026-06-06). B8 shipped as CTA-only cta-pair activation by user decision; this entry records that the literal 'emit all section.buttons' intent of B8's cited lines (_fills_hero/_fills_text_block/etc.) is NOT achievable for single-CTA-slot seeds, and the real instances are a classification problem. Related: phase-53-b8-cta-pair-color-fidelity, phase-53-a2-advisory-section-gate. CLOSED 2026-06-11 (commit 5d0b2bdb): the original 'classification/segmentation problem' framing was WRONG. LCA analysis of case 10 sec#1 showed it is a correctly-segmented single mj-column (mj-wrapper > mj-section > mj-column > [text, text, btn, btn]); the section count is right (case 7 -> 17, case 10 -> 14, unchanged), so this is a FILL bug, not segmentation. text-block has no 'CTA slot' — _fills_text_block appended buttons[0] as inline HTML into the body slot; the fix loops over every non-placeholder button. Also fixed the white-on-white the loop surfaced: outlined buttons (stroke present — case 10 text_color #010101 + stroke #FE5219; case 7 #000000) were forced color:#ffffff and rendered invisible; stroke-gated, they now emit their designed text_color + border (solid-fill buttons keep hardcoded white — see phase-53-b8-text-block-solid-cta-text-color). A2 ladder untouched (no count change). Guard: TestButtonInTextBlock.test_text_block_renders_all_buttons (RED pre-fix on button count AND colour). Baselines 7,10 regenerated (diff-audited intended-vs-structural); cases 5,6,8,9 byte-identical. Gates green: types 0, golden-conformance 26, design_sync+components 2691, make test 8163.

items.title: _fills_cta keys on button count, not slug — can desync from routing on the VLM fallback path
items.summary: B8 made _fills_cta branch on len(section.buttons)>=2 (emit primary_/secondary_ for cta-pair) vs single (cta_text/cta_url for cta-button). The component dispatch maps BOTH slugs to the same _fills_cta (component_matcher.py:515,545), and the filler is NOT told which slug/seed it is filling. On the normal path this is safe because _match_by_type and _fills_cta encode the SAME >=2 condition, so slug and fills always agree. But match_section_with_vlm_fallback (~145/166) sets the slug from the VLM result (vlm_result.component_type) independent of button count, then calls _build_slot_fills(slug, section). If the VLM picked 'cta-button' for a 2-button section, _fills_cta emits primary_/secondary_ into the cta-button seed (slots cta_text/cta_url) → both fills are no-ops → button labels lost + cta-button placeholder leaks. The reverse (VLM 'cta-pair' for 1 button) leaks the 'Primary button'/'Secondary button' seed placeholders. Before B8, _fills_cta always emitted cta_text/cta_url, so a VLM 'cta-button' pick was self-consistent; B8 introduced the desync.
items.symptom_if_broken: With DESIGN_SYNC__VLM_FALLBACK_ENABLED=true and a VLM-wired pipeline: a low-confidence non-CTA section reclassified by the VLM to a cta slug renders with empty/placeholder buttons ('Primary button'/'Secondary button' leak, or dropped labels).
items.closes_when: Either _fills_cta becomes slug-aware (thread the chosen slug into the filler and emit the slot set matching the seed), or the dispatch keeps cta-button/cta-pair on separate fillers. Verified: a section forced to slug 'cta-button' with 2 buttons emits cta_text/cta_url (not primary_/secondary_), and slug 'cta-pair' with 1 button does not leak pair placeholders.
items.notes: Found by adversarial review during B8 execution (2026-06-06). DOUBLY UNREACHABLE today: (1) vlm_fallback_enabled defaults to False (app/core/config/design_sync.py:47), and (2) match_section_with_vlm_fallback has NO non-test callers — it is not wired into the live convert_document pipeline. CTA sections also return confidence 1.0, so even an enabled VLM fallback never fires for them; the only trigger is a low-confidence NON-CTA section the VLM reclassifies to a cta slug. Logged as speculative so it surfaces if/when VLM fallback is wired up. Related: phase-53-b8-cta-pair-color-fidelity, phase-53-b8-non-cta-multibutton-drop. CLOSED 2026-06-11 (commit 2bb6bdbf): _fills_cta now keys the slot set on the chosen slug threaded through _build_slot_fills (cta-pair → primary_*/secondary_* with empty fills blanking the seed when <2 buttons; text-link → link_text/link_url, fixing the same desync class found during preflight; other CTA slugs → cta_text/cta_url). Guard: TestFillsCtaSlugAware in test_cta_fidelity.py, 4/5 proven RED pre-fix. Cases 5-10 byte-identical.

items.title: Solid-fill text-block CTAs hardcode white label text, ignoring ButtonElement.text_color
items.summary: _fills_text_block renders a CTA's inline <a> with color:#ffffff for solid-fill (non-stroked) buttons, ignoring the extracted ButtonElement.text_color. The phase-53-b8-non-cta-multibutton-drop fix deliberately gated the designed-text-colour + border path on stroke presence (the unambiguous 'outlined button' signal) because solid-fill text_color is often a Figma text-node default rather than visual intent. White-on-coloured is the conventional readable choice and matches intent for case 6 (white on #006241 green) and case 8 (white on #DB291B red). BUT case 5 (maap) 'Discover ->' is a solid #0066cc blue button whose extracted design text_color is #010101 (black) — we render white, diverging from the data. White vs black cannot be adjudicated without the design render (reference PNGs are not committed to git; plan §2.3).
code_refs: app/design_sync/component_matcher.py:_fills_text_block (fg defaults to '#ffffff'; designed text_color used only when btn.stroke_color + btn.stroke_weight present)
data/debug/5 (case 5 'Discover ->': solid #0066cc fill, design text_color #010101, rendered white-on-blue)
items.symptom_if_broken: A solid-fill text-block CTA whose design specifies dark label text renders white-on-colour instead; case 5's button shows white text where the extracted design data says #010101. Low-contrast or wrong-colour labels on non-outlined buttons in real designs.
items.closes_when: Visual verification (a committed design render or a VLM compare) resolves whether case 5's solid button is white- or dark-labelled; _fills_text_block either adopts text_color for solid buttons too, or documents white as the deliberate override. Verified by case 5's rendered label colour matching the confirmed design.
items.notes: Deliberate scope boundary of the phase-53-b8-non-cta-multibutton-drop fix (commit 5d0b2bdb): outlined buttons (stroke) use designed text_color + border; solid buttons keep white. Recorded because the chosen white diverges from extracted data on case 5 and can't be adjudicated without a design render — blocked on the same asset re-export as plan §A3/§A4. Related: phase-53-b8-non-cta-multibutton-drop, phase-53-b8-cta-pair-color-fidelity.
items.resolution: FIXED per closes_when, adjudicated from raw design data instead of the blocked render re-export: c7 raw_figma mj-button 2833:1890 (fill #FFFFFF, stroke #000000/2.0 strokeAlign INSIDE, cornerRadius 25) with label TEXT 2833:1891 fill #000000 proves outlined buttons are dark-labelled by design — extracted button text fills are visual intent, not Figma text-node defaults, killing the 'often a Figma default' premise that justified the white hardcode. _fills_text_block's stroke-less branch now threads _safe_color(btn.text_color, '#ffffff') — white only as absence-fallback — mirroring _column_cta_row:842; the outlined branch is byte-unchanged (keeps its border + darker #1a1a1a absence-fallback). Case 5 'Discover ->' (raw TEXT 2833:1640 fill #000000; this entry's #010101 was the pre-re-audit fixture value, superseded by the 2026-07-05 exact-raw re-audit; extracted fill_color is None -> renderer-default #0066cc, not a design blue) now renders color:#000000 — the label matches the confirmed design. c6 'Order your fall favorite' raw TEXT 2833:1439 fill #FFFFFF confirms white IS the c6 design (byte-level no-op beyond hex case). Baselines diff-audited: c5 1 semantic line; c6 1 case-only line (#ffffff -> #FFFFFF, extracted case flows through, column-path parity); c8 2 case-only 'DISCOVER IT' lines (the regen's trailing-ws churn is stripped by the pre-commit hook — the churn's actual origin); c7/c9/c10 byte-identical (c9's dark-label 'WATCH THE VIDEO' never renders — pre-existing template-path drop, out of scope). White-on-white grep (background-color:#FFFFFF;color:#ffffff, any case) zero on all 6; ladder 13/9/8/10/8/12 held; fidelity scores flat to 3dp on all 18 metrics. Gates: types 0-err, design_sync+components 2854, backend 8294, golden 26, snapshot 34/10sk/1xf. Guarded by TestTextBlockCTALabelColor (test_cta_fidelity.py): dark-label-on-light-fill RED-proven pre-fix (failure output was the exact background-color:#FFFFFF;color:#ffffff invisible signature); white-fallback-when-text_color-absent pins the absence-fallback against _safe_color's #333333 default. Own-row CTA emission (vs inline-in-body) deliberately not taken — 51.1 territory.

items.title: LLM-judge calibration is uncertified on the synthetic corpus - only 2 criteria are structurally calibratable and both miss TPR>=0.85
items.summary: Phase 54.1 built the reward corpus, but real LLM-judge calibration is limited by corpus composition. 84% of verdicts are deterministic QA-backed (verifiable, trivially TPR/TNR=1.0 - NOT judge calibration). Only scaffolder/brief_fidelity and dark_mode/color_coherence have both-class LLM-judge data; against independent blind bootstrap labels they land at TPR 0.62/TNR 0.86 and TPR 0.80/TNR 1.00 - TNR meets target, TPR does not. personalisation/logic_match is vacuous (empty-brief component fixtures), scaffolder/design_fidelity is degenerate auto-pass; both left unlabeled. The residual brief_fidelity disagreement is a criterion-definition ambiguity (terse 'snippet/effect' briefs the judge wants as full HTML), not a clear judge defect. Bootstrap labels are model-assisted, provisional, pending human review.
items.symptom_if_broken: Activating BLUEPRINT__JUDGE_ON_RETRY in Phase 54.2 on these uncertified judges feeds recovery retries an over-strict reward signal -> acceptable outputs get rejected -> extra iterations / degraded output. recovery_ledger uses a QA-outcome reward and is far less exposed.
items.closes_when: LLM-judge TPR/TNR reach >=0.85/0.80 on an agent-relevant labeled set (real briefs + human-reviewed labels), e.g. once EVAL__PRODUCTION_SAMPLE_RATE accumulates on-policy production verdicts and a human-label pass replaces the provisional bootstrap.
items.notes: Phase 54.1 verify criterion 'judges hit TPR>=0.85/TNR>=0.80' is met for TNR and for the verifiable-QA criteria, but NOT for the 2 real LLM-judge criteria on this corpus - surfaced honestly rather than manufactured. User chose the 'honest bootstrap' option in the 54.1 execution Q&A. Phase 54.2 honored this block: BLUEPRINT__JUDGE_ON_RETRY stayed default-OFF (not flipped). The now-removed BLUEPRINT__JUDGE_AGGREGATION_ENABLED loop used to persist the inline judge's verdict inside the judge_on_retry path; that verdict-persistence branch was surgically removed in 54.2 while the inline-judge path (inline_judge.py, gated by judge_on_retry) was preserved intact for a future activation once LLM-judge calibration is certified.

items.title: BrandRepair._repair_footer is dead as a compliance backstop — never reached from design-sync and never injects unsub links
items.summary: Audit-4 (RC-F5) found the comment claiming BrandRepair fixes footer legal/unsub content downstream is false. RepairPipeline is never invoked from any design-sync path (converter_service.py / import_service.py / routes.py have zero repair imports), and BrandRepair._repair_footer early-returns on any element matching class="...footer..." (the seed's footer-bg qualifies) and, even when it does fire, injects only a <footer> with copyright/address/legal_text from DesignSystem — never an unsubscribe LINK. F5 makes the converter self-sufficient instead: the email-footer seed's footer_legal cell (unsub/preferences/address rows) is preserved verbatim and never overwritten by _fills_footer.
code_refs: app/qa_engine/repair/brand.py:141-172 (BrandRepair._repair_footer — early-return at :149 on footer class; no unsub-link injection)
app/design_sync/converter_service.py (no RepairPipeline import — converter is self-sufficient by design, plan §1 F5 decision 4)
items.symptom_if_broken: A scaffolder-pipeline (non-converter) email whose template carries a decorative footer class ships missing an unsubscribe link — BrandRepair sees the class, assumes a footer exists, and never injects one.
items.closes_when: BrandRepair._repair_footer is either repaired to inject an unsub link when one is absent (not just a <footer> block) or removed as dead code, OR the scaffolder pipeline gains an equivalent always-present footer_legal guarantee.
items.notes: Adjacent, NOT covered by this entry: F5's footer_legal preserves the email-footer seed's hardcoded '© Company Name / 123 Business Street' — those are LITERALS, not merge tags, and nothing in the converter path replaces them (RepairPipeline deliberately not wired). That is within the plan's 'legal row never overwritten' decision (v1 dedupe is a non-goal; a design carrying its own unsub text yields both an editorial and a legal row). Per-brand legal-text substitution is a separate design-system concern.

items.title: Plain IMAGE decorations are indistinguishable from content photos at extraction
items.summary: Extraction sets is_background only for frame fills (layout_analyzer.py), so a plain IMAGE node that is decorative (arrow, pin, texture, thermometer) carries no signal distinguishing it from a content photo. The F1/F3 image-width work uses a size heuristic (<=64px never stretches; design-width threading) as a stopgap to keep small decorations from ballooning, but a genuinely large decorative image is still treated as content. The real fix is z-order / semantic-role capture at ingest (53.3d territory).
code_refs: app/design_sync/figma/layout_analyzer.py:105 (is_background field), :1296 (set True only on frame fills)
app/design_sync/figma/layout_analyzer.py:1318 (frame-wrapping-single-image branch uses node.width = the FRAME width, inflating a small wrapped image to its column width — the c10 arrow case)
items.symptom_if_broken: A large decorative IMAGE (arrow, texture, badge) renders at content size or stacks as if it were a content photo, because nothing flags it decorative and the size heuristic only catches small icons.
items.closes_when: Ingest captures z-order / role so decorative IMAGE nodes are flagged distinctly from content photos (Phase 53.3d), AND the frame-wrap branch (layout_analyzer.py:1318) uses the inner image's own width when it is much smaller than its wrapper — replacing the size heuristic.
items.notes: Surfaced by audit-4 §RC-F7 / de-ranked findings; the heuristic is deliberately a stopgap, not the fix. F3 execution (2026-07-05) CONFIRMED the specific mammut (c10) mechanism: the MEN/WOMEN/EQUIPMENT red nav arrows are 28x28 IMAGE nodes wrapped in 268px mj-image frames, so layout_analyzer.py:1318 reports img.width=268 (== column) — F3's img.width-based rule (matcher/renderer scope) cannot shrink them because the width it reads is already wrong. User ratified deferring c10 to this entry; F3 fixed only the correctly-sized c9 icons (34/48px). The real fix is ingest-side (frame-wrap width + role capture).

items.title: F6 eyebrow row can steal a section's partial _cell padding from the heading
items.summary: F6 splices pre-heading eyebrow <tr><td data-node-id> rows above the heading row and uses a padding-bottom LONGHAND so the section _cell padding override (_replace_first_css_prop on the `padding:` shorthand) still lands on the heading <td>, not the eyebrow. This dodge only covers 4-side (shorthand) section padding. When a section has PARTIAL padding, _build_token_overrides emits per-side _cell longhands applied via _upsert_first_td_css_prop, which targets the FIRST <td> in the HTML — after the splice that is the eyebrow row, so the heading loses its intended per-side padding to the eyebrow. Both corpus acceptance cases (c5/c8) use 4-side padding, so this is unexercised.
items.symptom_if_broken: A text-block/hero section with a pre-heading eyebrow AND partial (non-4-side) section padding renders the heading with the eyebrow's padding and the eyebrow row carrying the heading's intended per-side padding.
items.closes_when: _upsert_first_td_css_prop is scoped to the heading/first-content <td> (skipping spliced eyebrow rows), or _cell per-side longhands target data-slot cells explicitly. Verified by a partial-padding eyebrow section rendering the heading padding correctly.
items.notes: Speculative gap found by inspection during F6 (RC-F6). No corpus fixture carries partial padding + eyebrow. Also unhandled by F6: grouped / multi-column eyebrows (flat single-col path only) — no corpus fixture either.

items.summary: The text-block CTA-append path in _fills_text_block emits a hardcoded `border-radius:4px` for every button, discarding btn.border_radius (which the document model carries correctly). The sibling column path _column_cta_row honors btn.border_radius with a '4' fallback. Surfaced during the RC-F7 pill-radius fixture recovery (fix/phase-53f-f7-pills-radius): c5 section-[1] 'Discover →' carries border_radius=25.0 (verified via from_legacy) yet renders 4px; corpus-wide the same hardcode forces every text-block CTA that has a design radius to 4px — confirmed on c6 'Order your fall favorite' and c7 'Explore now' (both 25.0 in the model, 4px in expected.html). c5's 7 COLUMN city pills route through _column_cta_row and correctly render 25px, which is why the fixture patch alone recovered them.
code_refs: app/design_sync/component_matcher.py:843 (_column_cta_row — the honor-radius pattern to mirror: radius = f'{btn.border_radius:.0f}' if not None else '4')
items.symptom_if_broken: A text-block section CTA renders square (border-radius:4px) even when the design specifies a rounded/pill radius. Concretely c5 'Discover →', c6 'Order your fall favorite', c7 'Explore now' show 4px instead of their 25px design radius.

User-ratified (2026-07-05) to recover only the 7 c5 column pills via the fixture patch and DEFER this shared render-path fix, because fixing it moves the c6/c7 baselines (they are NOT byte-identical after) and belongs in its own scoped effort. Companion to phase-53f-f7-stale-case5-fixture-drops-pill-radius (closed by the fixture patch) — that was the fixture staleness; this is the residual render-path bug that keeps the section-[1] Discover pill square.
items.resolution: FIXED (branch fix/phase-53f-app-render-field-parity) exactly per closes_when: _fills_text_block threads btn.border_radius with fallback '4' mirroring _column_cta_row (component_matcher.py); c5/c6/c7 expected.html regenerated and diff-audited — exactly 3 changed lines, all text-block CTA border-radius 4px->25px ('Discover ->', 'Order your fall favorite', 'Explore now'); c8 regen churn was trailing-whitespace-only and reverted; c9/c10 byte-identical; ladder 13/9/8/10/8/12 held (snapshot regression 34 passed/1 xfail). items.title: RC-F7 card wrapper's relocated _cell padding overflows the 600px MSO ghost in Outlook
items.summary: _wrap_col_bg_inner_card inserts a width=100% card table whose <td> becomes the first cell, so the section's _cell padding override (component_matcher.py:2169, applied after inner_bg -> deterministic) lands 20px on the WRAPPER cell, which sits OUTSIDE the MSO ghost (<table width=600>). In the Word/Outlook engine the card is: 100% wrapper -> td with 40px horizontal padding -> a fixed 600px ghost inside a 560px content box; Word will not shrink the ghost, so the card overflows the 600px email column (~640px). Chromium honours the 100% wrapper and renders correctly; the fidelity scorer (Chromium) + DOM/string unit tests + make test never exercise the MSO path, so no gate caught it. Same class as the F3 Outlook attr/width latent.
code_refs: app/design_sync/component_matcher.py:2169 (_cell padding override, hits the wrapper cell post-wrap)
items.symptom_if_broken: In Outlook (Word engine) a LEGO column-layout card renders ~40px too wide, overflowing the 600px email column / misaligning the columns. Chromium-correct.
items.closes_when: The 51.1 card-padding restructure (lime-float + uniform inner-card inset) threads card padding INSIDE the MSO ghost cells (per-column) instead of onto the width=100% wrapper cell, so the 600px ghost fits, and c7 is re-rendered + Outlook-verified.
items.notes: Non-blocking: converter fidelity is Chromium-scored and c7 0.636->0.719 is the Chromium win. Logged per the no-silent-caps discipline (cf. F3's Outlook attr/width note). Belongs with the 51.1 lime-float/card-padding work, which restructures this region anyway. A quick Outlook-safe stopgap (drop the wrapper td's style=padding:0 so _cell stays on col_1 inside the ghost) was considered but reintroduces the pre-fix image/text top misalignment and lowers the Chromium score, so deferred to the holistic 51.1 fix.

items.title: Column seeds' fixed 600px-context widths overflow padded content boxes — 2-col layouts render stacked
items.summary: column-layout-{2,3,4} seeds hardcode per-column pixel widths sized for a full 600px context (MSO ghost td width=300/200/150 + div max-width). Any horizontal inset — a band's _cell padding (24px/side), RC-F7's relocated card padding (20px/side) — shrinks the live content box below the seed total (c7 cards: ~512px vs 600px of columns), so the display:inline-block column divs wrap and multi-column sections render stacked. _apply_column_width_fractions (A8) redistributes the seed total by measured fractions but never RESCALES the total to the effective box; detection is CORRECT (sections match column-layout-* — plan 53-f §4 row 4's 'column-detection widening' is the wrong lever). Dominant remaining c7 full_image mechanism at Track-F close-out.
items.symptom_if_broken: Any multi-column section inside a padded band or card renders its columns stacked vertically instead of side-by-side, and the email renders far taller than the design (c7 app export 4470px vs 3223px design; c7 all 6 benefit cards image-above-text; c7 user-info col4 wraps its 4th cell; c8 2x2 spec grid single-column; c10 product grid single-column).
items.closes_when: The renderer rescales column ghost td widths + div max-widths so their sum fits the effective content box (600 minus accumulated horizontal insets), preserving A8 fractions; c7/c8/c10 baselines regenerated + diff-audited showing side-by-side columns; ladder unchanged (13/9/8/10/8/12).
items.notes: Diagnosed 2026-07-05 at Track-F close-out (plan 53-f §6 close-out; composite-verified on harness + a LEGO app export vs the re-audited P50 target). F9-class renderer slice — no non-MJML fixture dependency. Interacts with phase-53f-f7-card-wrapper-outlook-ghost-overflow (both resolve in how column/card widths thread through the MSO ghost). CLOSED 2026-07-06 (e6812b44, fix/phase-53f9-column-width-budget, plan 53-f9-column-width-budget.md): _shrink_column_ghost_widths rescales ghost td widths + div max-widths + the ghost table total together (A8 fractions + all-or-nothing preserved) at two call sites — render_section 3c (card wrapper inset) and render_repeating_group rows (2x band horizontal). closes_when met in full: c5/c7/c8/c9/c10 baselines regenerated + diff-audited width-only (c6 byte-identical; c8 sec[9] un-inset control untouched), composites show c7 cards side-by-side / c8 spec grid 2x2 / c10 grid 2-up, ladder 13/9/8/10/8/12 held. c7 0.771->0.794, c10 0.678->0.742. The Outlook ghost-overflow sibling STAYS OPEN (arithmetic now fits 512+88=600 but unverified in the Word engine).

items.title: _build_column_fill_html emits images->texts->buttons buckets, discarding design y-order
items.summary: The column fill builder iterates group.images, then group.texts, then group.buttons, so in-column content renders category-bucketed instead of in design vertical order. A tag pill (a filled rect + label, classified as a ButtonElement) renders below the body text when the design places it above the heading — F6's eyebrow lift covers pre-heading TEXTS only — and product-card spec icons (images) render above the product name.

items.notes: Diagnosed 2026-07-05 at Track-F close-out (plan 53-f §6 close-out, composite-verified). Pairs with phase-53f-f7-text-block-cta-hardcoded-radius / phase-53-b8-text-block-solid-cta-text-color for the full pill/CTA fidelity story.

items.title: App-side conversions cannot reach harness fidelity: snapshot _file_structure cache + protocol DesignNode bridge drop the Track-B render fields (stroke/radius/text-align; text_color dies downstream)
items.summary: Validating the ceiling-doc §3 ops caveat end-to-end (2026-07-06, imports 39/41 on connection 5) disproved its remedy: after a fresh connection re-sync (snapshot 20, fresh _file_structure with text_color) and cache-free conversions, the LEGO 'Explore now' CTA still renders white-on-white, borderless, r4 — byte-identical output (70686B) to the stale 2026-04 render — while the harness (same renderer code, data/debug/7/structure.json) renders black-on-white + 2px border + r4. Mechanism: run_conversion's legacy path consumes protocol DesignNodes rebuilt from snapshot.tokens_json._file_structure via cached_dict_to_node, whose schema has NO corner_radius/stroke_weight/stroke_color/text_align (the sync serializer never persists them either — fresh snapshot has 0 occurrences), and text_color survives the cache but is dropped in the downstream protocol->converter bridge (fresh-vs-March snapshot outputs are byte-identical). The harness bypasses all of this by loading structure.json with the converter-native deserializer.
code_refs: app/design_sync/import_service.py:270 (legacy path: normalize_tree + EmailDesignDocument.from_legacy on protocol nodes)
items.symptom_if_broken: Any app-side design import renders CTAs/pills without stroke, radius, per-node text color or alignment (e.g. invisible white-on-white outlined buttons), even immediately after a connection re-sync — while the fixture harness scores the same design correctly. Users judge the converter far below its harness numbers.
items.closes_when: An app-side POST /imports conversion of the LEGO frame (connection-5 class data) renders 'Explore now' with text_color + stroke parity with data/debug/7/expected.html (r25 additionally needs phase-53f-f7-text-block-cta-hardcoded-radius), guarded by a test that round-trips a DesignNode with stroke/radius/text-align through the snapshot cache serializer and the protocol->converter bridge.
items.notes: Found while executing the 2026-07-06 asset-restore ops task. Same inert-serializer-bridge class as RC-A/RC-B (Phase 52). Validation artifacts left in the dev DB: imports 39 (tmpl 42, pre-sync) and 41 (tmpl 44, post-sync forced-legacy via nulled snapshot-20 document_json) — both byte-identical to stale tmpl 41 (import 38, 2026-07-05). Stale tmpl 28 (import 26, 2026-04-19) additionally lacks text-align/centering. The ceiling doc §3 ops caveat is amended to point here.
items.resolution: FIXED same day (branch fix/phase-53f-app-render-field-parity). Two mechanisms, one of them mis-hypothesized at filing: (1) serialize_node/cached_dict_to_node extended to round-trip every DesignNode render field (corner_radius, corner_radii, stroke_weight, stroke_color, text_align, primary/counter_axis_align, style_runs, image_ref, hyperlink, line_height_relative, visible, opacity) — guarded by test_serialization_roundtrip.py incl. a dataclass-fields parity test that fails when DesignNode grows a field the cache pair does not carry. (2) The 'text_color dies in the protocol->converter bridge' hypothesis was WRONG — normalize_tree/_filter_structure use dataclasses.replace (lossless) and ButtonElement carried #000000 correctly; the label was repainted by the _sanitize_email_html post-pass: _fix_text_contrast treated only table/td/tr as background carriers and started ranges after the opening tag, so an inline CTA's own white pill background was invisible and its style-attr color was judged against the dark band. Fixed by adding 'a' to the bg-tag set and starting ranges at the opening tag; guarded by test_contrast_fix_outlined_button_own_light_bg_preserved / test_contrast_fix_dark_button_own_bg_still_corrected. End-to-end closes_when met: POST /connections/sync (snapshot 21, stroke x277/corner_radius x631/text_align x2615 in _file_structure) -> import 43 -> tmpl 46 renders 'Explore now' background #FFFFFF / color #000000 / border 2px solid #000000 / border-radius 25px with the heading text-align:center — full parity with data/debug/7/expected.html (r25 via the text-block radius fix, same branch). Screenshot-verified on the purple band.

items.title: run_conversion document path ignores selected_node_ids — converts the whole design file when snapshot.document_json exists
items.summary: When the latest DesignTokenSnapshot carries document_json (a fresh POST /connections/sync now writes one — 691KB for connection 5), run_conversion prefers EmailDesignDocument.from_json(snapshot.document_json) and converts it WITHOUT filtering to the import's selected_node_ids. Observed 2026-07-06: import 40 (145 selected LEGO nodes) produced a 2,056,671-byte template (tmpl 43) containing zero occurrences of 'Explore now'/'LEGO'/any of the email's content — the whole community-file document was converted instead of the selected frame.
code_refs: app/design_sync/import_service.py:203 (document path: from_json(snapshot.document_json), no node filtering)
items.symptom_if_broken: After any connection re-sync on current code, every subsequent design import converts the entire file into a multi-MB template with none of the selected email's content — looks like total converter failure but is a path-selection bug.
items.closes_when: The document path filters the document to the import's selected_node_ids (or run_conversion falls back to the legacy path when selected_node_ids is non-empty), with a test converting a selected-nodes import against a multi-frame snapshot document.
items.notes: Ops workaround used for the 2026-07-06 validation and LEFT IN PLACE for connection 5: snapshot 20's document_json set to NULL in the dev DB so the legacy path engages (a future sync will regenerate document_json and re-expose this bug). Blocks meaningful use of the fresh document_json snapshots that re-sync now produces.
items.resolution: FIXED same day (branch fix/phase-53f-app-render-field-parity) via the closes_when's sanctioned fallback: run_conversion takes the snapshot document path only when design_import.selected_node_ids is empty; selected-nodes imports use the legacy path, which filters via _filter_structure. Guarded by test_run_conversion_selected_nodes_skip_document_path (RED-proven on pre-fix code) + test_run_conversion_whole_file_import_uses_document_path (document path preserved for whole-file imports). Live-proven: snapshot 21 HAS document_json, yet imports 42/43 (145 selected LEGO nodes) produced the 71,869-byte LEGO email, not the 2MB whole-file output. The interim dev-DB workaround (snapshot 20 document_json nulled) is obsolete but harmless — snapshot 21 supersedes it.

tooling_followups.title: Wire deferred-items.json scan into /preflight-check
tooling_followups.trigger: When open_items count reaches >= 5 (was 5 when this entry was opened; closed at 6)
tooling_followups.summary: Add a step to .claude/commands/preflight-check.md that grep s .agents/deferred-items.json for entries whose phase or code_refs overlap the plan's 'Files to Create/Modify' list, and surfaces matches in the preflight report. This converts the convention from 'rule the model must remember' to 'mechanical check that runs before /be-execute'.
tooling_followups.acceptance: Preflight report includes a 'Deferred Items Touching This Plan' section listing matching entries with id, summary, and severity. Empty section when no matches — never silent.
tooling_followups.resolution: Step 2 'Deferred-Items Cross-Reference' added to .claude/commands/preflight-check.md (with 2a phase-match and 2b code_refs-match jq queries); Step 6 report template gained a 'Deferred Items Touching This Plan' table that is required to render even when empty.
